title: cardamine hirsuta subsp. oligosperma
intro: Cardamine hirsuta subsp. oligosperma, also known as hairy bittercress, is a perennial plant species in the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Hairy bittercress is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be consumed raw in salads or used as a cooking herb, although its use in modern herbal preparations is limited due to a lack of scientific research.
